sets.title: PBR Channel Roles
sets.instruction: Match each Principled BSDF input to what it controls

pairs.def: Defines the surface's visible color and pattern — connect an sRGB Image Texture here
Controls how matte or glossy the surface appears — 0 is a mirror, 1 is chalk
Switches the surface between metallic reflection (1) and non-metallic (0) — usually a binary value
Simulates surface bumps and grooves by changing how light strikes the mesh — no extra geometry needed

sets.title: Map Types
sets.instruction: Match each map type to how it creates surface detail

pairs.def: Grayscale height values — fakes depth using light and shadow only, no real geometry changes
Stores surface angle direction as RGB data — dense detail with zero extra polygons
Actually pushes and pulls mesh vertices — real geometric depth, requires Subdivision Surface modifier
Per-pixel gloss control — dark (0) is smooth and mirror-like, light (1) is rough and matte

sets.title: Color Spaces
sets.instruction: Match each texture type to its correct color space setting in Blender

pairs.def: sRGB — stores perceptual color data the way human eyes see it; needed for correct color appearance
Non-Color — raw linear data; wrong setting makes surfaces look muddy and incorrect
Non-Color — angle data encoded linearly; gamma correction would corrupt the surface normals
Non-Color — binary mask (0 or 1); no perceptual color interpretation needed

sets.title: Node Connections
sets.instruction: Match each node or setup to its purpose in the Shader Editor

pairs.def: Required between a normal map Image Texture and the Normal input — decodes the RGB angle data correctly
Converts a grayscale height map into normal direction data compatible with the Normal input
Correct color space for roughness, metallic, and normal map data textures — do not use sRGB for these
Required pair for displacement maps to work — subdivision provides the extra vertices the map pushes

sets.title: Workflow & Tools
sets.instruction: Match each action or tool to its role in the materials workflow

pairs.def: Embeds all linked textures inside the .blend file — critical step before uploading to Canvas
Free CC0 PBR texture library — each download includes albedo, roughness, normal, and displacement maps
Blender's main physically-based shader — consolidates all PBR channels (color, roughness, metallic, normal) in one node

pairs.term: Moving a texture file after linking it
pairs.def: Breaks the material — Blender stores file paths, not the files themselves; moved files cause missing texture errors
